L1.area: Development tools and database profiling utilities

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled data submitted via a POST request is deserialized and each embedded query string is passed directly to a database query execution method without sanitization or access control, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary SQL queries.

L3.area: Database query profiling tool in the PHP development assets
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled data submitted via a POST request to the database query profiling endpoint is deserialized and each embedded query string is passed directly to a database explain-statement execution method without sanitization or authentication checks, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary SQL queries against any configured database.

ground_truth.reason: The profile.php endpoint accepts POST data containing serialized query strings and passes them directly to the database ExplainStatement method without any sanitization or authentication, allowing unauthenticated SQL injection.
